title: Ecological Transition Invests â‚¬1.214 Billion in Renewable Hydrogen Clusters
content: Spainâ€™s Ministry of Ecological Transition (MITECO) has allocated â‚¬1.214 billion from NextGenEU funds to develop seven renewable hydrogen clusters across Aragon, Andalusia, Castile and Leon, Catalonia, and Galicia. These projects, with a combined electrolysis capacity of 2,278 MW, will generate 9,000 direct and 11,000 indirect jobs, mobilizing â‚¬4.907 billion in investments. The clusters will integrate production, storage, and distribution of green hydrogen and derived fuels like e-methanol and ammonia, primarily using alkaline electrolysis technology. They align with Spainâ€™s Renewable Hydrogen Roadmap, targeting 12 GW of electrolysis capacity by 2030, accelerating industrial decarbonization and reducing fossil fuel reliance.
author: Analysts Account
